segments.text: And when the people complained, it displeased the LORD:
and the LORD heard it; and his anger was kindled;
and the fire of the LORD burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the uttermost parts of the camp.

footnotes: complained: or, were as it were complainers
it displeased: Heb. it was evil in the ears of

segments.text: And the people cried unto Moses;
when Moses prayed unto the LORD, the fire was quenched.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And he called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the LORD burnt among them.
And the mixt multitude that was among them fell a lusting:
Who shall give us flesh to eat?
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: We remember the fish, which we did eat in Egypt freely; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick:
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: But now our soul is dried away: there is nothing at all, beside this manna, before our eyes.
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And the LORD came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave it unto the seventy elders:
and it came to pass, that, when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.
Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the LORD's people were prophets, and that the LORD would put his spirit upon them!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And there went forth a wind from the LORD, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day's journey on this side, and as it were a day's journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.

footnotes: as it were a day's…: Heb. as it were the way of a day
heading: Quail Provision

segments.text: And he called the name of that place Kibroth-hattaavah: because there they buried the people that lusted.

footnotes: Kibroth-hattaavah: that is, The graves of lust
